It's not surprising to know that it was the only facility available, because no one in their right mind would actually choose to play there. The facility itself was dark, dingy, and dirty. There were multiple ceiling tiles that were removed and/or missing, and pink insulation was hanging out of the ceiling, dangling down towards the floor. The ceiling tiles that remained showed marks of significant water damage. The ceiling where the indoor courts were had some sort of paint or spray on the rafters, presumably keeping asbestos at bay. The courts themselves had several spots of uneven footing, small little sink holes throughout. Prior to us playing, a worker was going around and picking up multiple pieces of trash that had been scattered throughout the indoor courts. The bathrooms appeared as though they had not been cleaned in quite some time. They were musty and gross, but there was also what appeared to be feces stains on the toilet and just a general look of overall uncleanliness. I am not sure how this place has not been shut down for health code violations. The carpet looks as though it was something out of a 1980's skating rink and probably hasn't been cleaned-- shampooed or vacuumed-- since that time. When I googled the facility I came to find out it was an actual fitness center that people pay money to attend! I don't know why anyone would pay anything to go anywhere near this place. Even the driveway pulling up to the facility is in complete disrepair with large pot holes everywhere. The facility needs a 100% tear down and remodel.
